contentBlocks.original:
सरस्वती महाभद्रा महामाया वरप्रदा ।
श्रीप्रदा पद्मनिलया पद्माक्षी पद्मवक्त्रिगा ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarasvatī mahābhadrā mahāmāyā varapradā ।
śrīpradā padmanilayā padmākṣī padmavaktrigā ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Saraswati, the greatly auspicious, the great illusion, the granter of boons; the bestower of prosperity, dwelling in the lotus, lotus-eyed, lotus-faced.

contentBlocks.original:
शिवानुजा पुस्तकहस्ता ज्ञानमुद्रा रमा च वै ।
कामरूपा महाविद्या महापातकनाशिनी ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivānujā pustakahastā jñānamudrā ramā cha vai ।
kāmarūpā mahāvidyā mahāpātakanāśinī ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sister of Shiva, holding a book, displaying the gesture of wisdom, graceful; of desired form, great knowledge personified, destroyer of great sins.

contentBlocks.original:
महाश्रया मालिनी च महाभोगा महाभुजा ।
महाभागा महोत्साहा दिव्याङ्गा सुरवन्दिता ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāśrayā mālinī cha mahābhōgā mahābhujā ।
mahābhāgā mahōtsāhā divyāṅgā suravanditā ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The great refuge, garlanded, bestower of great enjoyment, of great arms; greatly fortunate, greatly enthusiastic, of divine form, adored by the gods.

contentBlocks.original:
महाकाली महापाशा महाकारा महाङ्कुशा ।
सीता च विमला विश्वा विद्युन्माला च वैष्णवी ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahākāḻī mahāpāśā mahākārā mahāṅkuśā ।
sītā cha vimalā viśvā vidyunmālā cha vaiṣṇavī ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Great Kali, great noose-holder, of great stature, wielding the great goad; Sita, the pure, all-pervading, wreathed in lightning, a form of Vishnu.

contentBlocks.original:
चन्द्रिका चन्द्रलेखाविभूषिता च महाफला ।
सावित्री सुरसादेवी दिव्यालङ्कारभूषिता ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chandrikā chandralēkhāvibhūṣitā cha mahāphalā ।
sāvitrī surasādēvī divyālaṅkārabhūṣitā ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chandrika (moonlight), adorned with a crescent, the greatly fruitful; Savitri, Goddess of the gods, adorned with divine ornaments.

contentBlocks.original:
वाग्देवी वसुधा तीव्रा महाभद्रा च भोगदा ।
गोविन्दा भारती भामा गोमती जटिला तथा ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāgdēvī vasudhā tīvrā mahābhadrā cha bhōgadā ।
gōvindā bhāratī bhāmā gōmatī jaṭilā tathā ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Goddess of speech, earth-sustaining, intense, the greatly auspicious, bestower of enjoyment; Govinda, Bharati, lustrous, Gomati, matted-haired.

contentBlocks.original:
विन्ध्यवासा चण्डिका च सुभद्रा सुरपूजिता ।
विनिद्रा वैष्णवी ब्राह्मी ब्रह्मज्ञानैकसाधना ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vindhyavāsā chaṇḍikā cha subhadrā surapūjitā ।
vinidrā vaiṣṇavī brāhmī brahmajñānaikasādhanā ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vindhya-dweller, Chandika, the auspicious, worshipped by gods; ever-wakeful, a form of Vishnu, Brahmi — the sole means to Brahma-knowledge.

contentBlocks.original:
सौदामिनी सुधामूर्ति स्सुवीणा च सुवासिनी ।
विद्यारूपा ब्रह्मजाया विशाला पद्मलोचना ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saudāminī sudhāmūrti ssuvīṇā cha suvāsinī ।
vidyārūpā brahmajāyā viśālā padmalōchanā ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lightning-flash, of pure nectar form, holding the fine veena, ever-pure; of the form of knowledge, Brahma's consort, wide-eyed, lotus-gazing.

contentBlocks.original:
शुम्भासुरप्रमथिनी धूम्रलोचनमर्दना ।
सर्वात्मिका त्रयीमूर्ति श्शुभदा शास्त्ररूपिणी ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śumbhāsurapramathinī dhūmralōchanamardanā ।
sarvātmikā trayīmūrti śśubhadā śāstrarūpiṇī ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Slayer of Shumbhasura, crusher of the smoke-eyed demon; the universal self, triple-form, the auspicious, embodiment of scriptures.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वदेवस्तुता सौया सुरासुरनमस्कृता ।
रक्तबीजनिहन्त्री च चामुण्डा मुण्डकाम्बिका ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvadēvastutā sauyā surāsuranamaskṛtā ।
raktabījanihantrī cha chāmuṇḍā muṇḍakāmbikā ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Praised by all gods, gentle, saluted by gods and demons; slayer of Raktabija, Chamunda, Mundakambika.

contentBlocks.original:
कालरात्रिः प्रहरणा कलाधारा निरञ्जना ।
वरारोहा च वाग्देवी वाराही वारिजासना ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāḻarātriḥ praharaṇā kaḻādhārā nirañjanā ।
varārōhā cha vāgdēvī vārāhī vārijāsanā ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kalaratri, bearer of weapons, foundation of the arts, the pure; the exalted one, Goddess of speech, a form of the boar, seated in the lotus.

contentBlocks.original:
चित्राम्बरा चित्रगन्धा चित्रमाल्यविभूषिता ।
कान्ता कामप्रदा वन्द्या रूपसौभाग्यदायिनी ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chitrāmbarā chitragandhā chitramālyavibhūṣitā ।
kāntā kāmapradā vandyā rūpasaubhāgyadāyinī ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Wearing varied garments, of varied fragrance, adorned with varied garlands; beloved, granter of desires, to be adored, bestower of beauty and good fortune.

contentBlocks.original:
श्वेतासना रक्तमध्या द्विभुजा सुरपूजिता ।
निरञ्जना नीलजङ्घा चतुर्वर्गफलप्रदा ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śvētāsanā raktamadhyā dvibhujā surapūjitā ।
nirañjanā nīlajaṅghā chaturvargaphalapradā ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: White-seated, red in the middle, two-armed, worshipped by the gods; the pure, blue-limbed, bestower of the fourfold fruits of life.

contentBlocks.original:
चतुराननसाम्राज्ञी ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवात्मिका ।
हंसानना महाविद्या मन्त्रविद्या सरस्वती ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturānanasāmrājñī brahmaviṣṇuśivātmikā ।
haṃsānanā mahāvidyā mantravidyā sarasvatī ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Empress of Brahma the four-faced, the embodiment of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva; swan-faced, great knowledge, mantra-knowledge, Saraswati herself.
Maha-Saraswati, knowledge of Tantra, devoted solely to the knowledge that liberates.
Thus the Saraswati Ashtottara Sata Nama Stotram is complete.
